title: Garbage and waste dumping and burning in vacant land
description:

Every evening this waste is burnt by some miscreant and leads to dense smoke and ashes in the air becoming a severe health hazard. Multiple complaints to BBMP has not resulted in any action. 

Our apartment ( 150+ residents ) is right beside this open site and are the most affected. Kids and elderly people are affected by serious health issues. There is also a school ( St.Teresa Convent ) and a hospital ( St.Mary Hospital ) opp and on the side of this site leading to a potential threat to school kids and hospital patients.

description:
Sir, 

We must live healthy life here in Bangalore.
Air pollution is big take on above point.

What can be done?
Solution:
As our BBMP commissioner rightly pointing out, if vehicles goes 10% every year what ever you build roads will never enough.

From this we can understand we must encourage people to use public transportation, why people are preferring to use personal vehicles instead of public transportation? Don't we have proper connectivity? Please check with the them , we can able to answer to all of them.

Things to follow to see Bangalore as clean City.
1. Create an imaginable understanding on usage of public transportation and to avoid air pollution , first in school , colleges, software office.

2. Advertise a short film on this.
3. The last mile connectivity can be done by walk to avoid future diabetes.
4. Please feel free to reach out to me or like minded students on pollution , so they can give all ideas for creating awareness.

We are uneducated literates, thats all the problem is.

title: Miserable condition of Panathur-Belagere Road Railway Underpass
description:
Panathur, due to its proximity to Whitefield and Sarjapura, has seen rapid growth in the last 2 years and is now filled with commercial properties and apartment complexes. Sobha Dream Acres, a massive undertaking on the Panathur-Belagere Road, has 6500 flats. Prestige Lake View, another apartment complex, has around 4000 flats to its name. All told there should be more than 15000 flats. 
Being extremely narrow, this road can barely handle two-way traffic. It is filled with garbage and potholes and is perpetually being dug up on some pretext. The icing on the cake is a narrow railway vent at the end of this stretch which allows only one-way traffic to commute at a time. 
The menace on the Panathur-Belagere Road begins even before sunrise at times and continues for the rest of the day. Over 20,000 vehicles pass through this road daily where only one side is let to travel at a time. The presence of a large number of water tankers only adds to the chaos as these tanker drivers rush along with a complete disregard for traffic or rules. 
Making matters worse, the traffic on the Panathur-Belagere Road is not being managed by the city’s traffic police. This Herculean task falls to private security guards deployed by some IT companies.  Many people don’t conform to the guard’s instructions as they have no legal power to penalize anyone. 
The residents of Panathur have been waiting for years, in suspended animation, hoping to see a ray of light in what seems to be a never-ending tunnel of darkness.
location: Panathuru Cirlce, Kadubeesanahalli, Panathur, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560103, India

title: BESCOM Power Cut Issues since past 3 years
description:
Respected Sir/Madam,

We have been observing BESCOM power cut of different category, and it has been going on for last 3 years, would have raised complaints in 100's (docket number of A category and N category), have met BESCOM officials, but nothing has helped. 

The nature of BESCOM power cut are like this:
1. Frequent BESCOM power cut (2 min, 5 min, 1 hrs, few hrs)
2. BESCOM power cut only in Purva Skywood, when BESCOM power exists in all the street lights and vicinity. 
3. 36 hrs BESCOM power cut (more than a day)
4. we have two phases in Purva Skywood, BESCOM power cut will happen in only one of the phases. 

During my last converstation (9 months back), i was told the area is being developed and will become model sub-division, BESCOM power will be provided with UG cable, we did see UG cable being laid out from HSR Layout to Silver County Road, but we continue to have BESCOM power issues.

As i write this complaint, we have another Frequent BESCOM power cut happening.

Please help.

Thanks
Anjanish
location: 92, Silver County Rd, Lakedew Residency- Phase 2, Harlur, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560068, India

title: BWSSB fails residents after collecting Kaver connectioni charges
description:
The residents and businesses, providing many jobs, are facing severe hardship due to no Kaveri water connection. Residents have been paying property taxes since 2006, yet we have not received any roads, or water connection. All Borewells in the layout, have gone dry and water tankers are charging exorbitant amounts to supply water. Last year the BWSSB issued notices and threatened, all residents and apartments, with blocking of sewage lines, if they did not apply and pay the charges to BWSSB for Kaveri water connection. Almost 60% owners and apartments have paid the demand charges of BWSSB. Others are waiting for BWSSB to put the Main Kaveri water line, into the layout, as they don't trust thr BWSSB, to keep their commitment. BWSSB, started the work and immediately stopped the laying of the main water line. When contacted, they are blaming the BBMP for not giving road cutting permission. This is all rubbish excuses. Even our local people representatives, are showing no concern for the hardship faced by the citizens. The situation, due to summer is now alarming. I urge our local leaders to kindly look into the matter and clear unnecessary bottlenecks, cause by people with vested interests. 

General Secretary SSLOA
location: Spice Garden Layout, Lakshminarayana Pura, Aecs Layout, Chinnapanna Halli, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560037, India

title: Roads full of dangerous pot holes, dug every 2 months and left with dangerously laid out gravel
description: I would like to raise a complaint against BBMP. Like, in the past 7-8 months, the roads in our locality undergo a digging and reconstruction regime almost every 2 months. It's a regular affair. The contractors do not take any responsibility for their activities, simply passing the ball onto their senior engineers.  The same story continues with the sr. engineers too. They often leave massive pot holes on the half dug, dangerously graveled roads. They cover it with more gravel to hide the potholes. They leave piles of dug up soil right in the middle of the road creating nuisance for people. There's hardly any space left to take vehicles (even 2 wheelers) out. Due to the constant dust in the air, my wife is now suffering with all sorts of dust allergies. We are planning to now move out of the city altogether because of such hostile living conditions. Before, we do, I really want to file a complaint against BBMP. Would be great if you could please let me know what is needed from my end.
location: 76, Nanjappa Garden, Horamavu, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560043, India

title: Please make Bangalore ORR safe for pedestrians
description:
Please construct skywalks (Foot Over-Bridges) across the Outer Ring Road from KR Puram Railway station to Sarjapur-ORR junction. Until skywalks are operational, please bring back traffic signals every one km. Otherwise we are not able to cross the road.

KR Puram-Iblur is a 12 km stretch with shopping malls, IT companies in big Tech parks, and apartments on both sides of the road. But there are ONLY TWO skywalks in the entire stretch. This is when we need a skywalk every 1 km so that people do not have to walk for more than 1 km just to cross the road (10 minutes walk).

Every single citizen in Bangalore who uses the ORR is aware of the problem, and the millions of people who live in apartments near ORR or who work in the huge number of IT companies on both sides of ORR are directly affected by this. 

Everyday, hundreds of pedestrians get hurt in Bangalore, some fatally. Daily, lakhs of people run for their lives while crossing the road. Please take this necessary step to make the city safe for pedestrians.
location: 3, Marathahalli - Sarjapur Outer Ring Rd, Devarabisanahalli, Bellandur, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560103, India

title: Storm water drain bridge is broken
description:
The bridge built on a storm water drain is almost 18 years old, All the Chambers and parts of the bridges are broken or have big cracks. This is a grave danger for everyone, tractors and huge vehicles are constantly moving on this bridge, weakening the structure with heavy rains and covered drain holes Making it worse. This morning, a tractor's rear end went into one of the chamber, breaking the chamber and weakening the structure. It's been open and the road has been blocked since then.
It smells so bad, and we realized that the drain is filled with garbage and mud and that's the reason the water flows on the bridge during heavy rainfall.
The residents of our area, would want the government to take necessary action as this is the only road for more than 20 houses, if it collapses, we will have no other way to even get out of our houses.
Please help us!

In 2015 we started construction with SBI verifying our site and approved for loan and ended in 2017. We have repeated sessions with AEEE, yet after that Muniraju still goes on doing this everytime the AEEE changes. description:
Today I was being caught by traffic police and the reason was bike silencer alteration. Since i don't wanted to roam to court so finally i made my mind to give bribe of 1500. Now due to the amendment of traffic fines this kind of cops increased their bribe rates. If a common man do mistakes then we get fine but what about this kind of cops. Govt is so much corrupt that only target to loot a common man like me and put all huge fines on a common man like me but no actions against these cops or corrupt people.
I know i did mistake by changing silencer but there should be atleast some warning with less fine if caught for first time. Plz don't make any amendment with a loop holes and in this amendment the biggest loop hole is corrupt cops. I am completely frustrated and lost hope on the system and governance. I request some higher officials to look into this issue and take action on that lady cop and help me to keep faith on the system. I know not all will be corrupted in the system. There will be some good people too because of which our country is still safe place to leave. Plz plz don't make me loose faith on my country because of only few bad people. title: Road is worst in IT hub
description: Neeladri Road in Electronics City Phase 1 is in race for the worst road in any urban area.Electronics City is the official IT hub of India’s IT Capital Bangalore. We all will have a general perception that this place will be super developed as it houses to the premium IT companies like Infosys, Wipro, HP etc. It is true to a certain extent. The roads are clean and developed inside electronics city but as soon as the road leaves the company area and enters to the residential area, the scene completely changes. nYes I am talking here about Neeladri Main Road, Electronics City Phase 1. This is road is not far from the Indian IT boom story, yet thoroughly neglected. This area is having a real estate boom with lots of premium apartments, restaurants, super markets, villas etc. but the road is a torture to the nearby residents and to whoever uses it to travel in and out of Electronics City. 378 bus route more pollution route and next to Mumbai City. At a first look you won’t believe this road exists in Bangalore that too in back yard of India’s premium IT hub.nRecently ELCITA developed the road till Wipro but afterwards the road condition is pathetic. The road is full of potholes, potholes and bigger potholes.nProblem faced by people in this arean• Jam packed traffic in the evening and morning even with lesser vehicles compared to other area of Bangalore.n• You cannot drive straight due to the pot holes resulting the opposite flow of traffic.n• Each evening you can see standstill traffic with dead blocks.n• As the vehicles travelling slowly due to the bigger potholes, the vehicles coming from behind are trying to overtake and causing the opposite flow to stop. n• Each vehicle is trying to overtake as there is no median or road divider marks. n• This road is a hell for pedestrians. No place to walk on the road as there is no foot path.n• Road is full of dust and it is very difficult to go even in two/four wheelers forget about pedestrians.n• Dangerous for children to catch their school busses. nI am no urban expert and not sure who is responsible for the development of the road. I am not sure if it is responsibility of Central Govt, State Govt, BDA, BBMP, ELCITA, Area MP or Area MLA. title: Extremely dangerous potholes @ service road
description:
Sir/Madam, 

This is umamaheswaran,  a father two daughters,  i stay at bommanhalli and i work in a comany at bellandur & i commute to work via silk board, hsr layout to reach bellandur circle.

I drive bike & take the usual route of taking the service road which is parallel to Fernhill apartments which is near silk board,  the service road is in a very poor condition that any vehicle is dangerous to drive,  i have been using this road since march 2019,  By using this service road it helps us to avoid silk board signal & take us bommnahalli directly, however i have personally had struggled & nearly three times avoided meeting an accident due to this pothole,  Every day has been a struggle for us & its been a nightmare as soon as we touch this service road. On Sep 6th 2019 while i return from my work,  i took the service road knowing the condition of the road & collapsed by hitting the pothole which start if the service road & fell down and my helmet face cover got damaged & the stones were literally tored of my face, now having a face with striches on  forehead, cheeks & the portion of left eye has been damaged and getting it treated now,  Both of my knee got striches & the scratches on my both hands, i am unable to walk or move my right hands now as it will take few weeks to heal slowly. 

My wife & two of my young daughters (6years old & 6 months baby) are completely depend on me,  we are trying to come out of this slowly.  Here our humble request is to look into this pothole of service road & kindly get it fixed before others meet such accidents in that place. 

Looking forward for your support to avoid further such accidents. 

Thanks. 

Regards, 
Umamaheswaran

title: Garbage collection is not happening properly in our locality
description:
Hi Team,

I reside at 3rd Main B-block, Vinayaka Nagar, Murgeshpalya, Vimanpura post, Bangalore-560017.
I have a severe complaint against garbage collection department as garbage collection is not happening properly in our locality. Wet waste is being collected once in a week and it's been more than 15 days dry waste have not been collected. This makes our locality look pathetic and unhygienic. Being working professionals we keep the garbage bins at the main entrance of our building before leaving for our respective works, and see no difference when we come back in evening. Earlier we were getting chance to communicate with garbage collectors over the weekends, but from last 2 weeks even that is not happening. They come once in a week (any random day) and would just collect wet waste. If by chance any one of us at home and get chance to question them for not collecting dry waste, they just give reason that they are gonna collect the same in sometime on the same day and they never show up. 
I really want to understand is there anything we are supposed to do? As instructed, we keep wet and dry waste in separate bins. 
I request you take action against this issue because i believe hygienist is the utmost thing required for an individual, society and the city.
Waiting for your quick action and support.

Attached is the pic of garbage bins not picked up from last 15 days.

Regards,
Khushboo 
Email: khushboo.111r@gmail.com

title: When you check the budget allocation for Ward no. 33, Manorayanap...
description:
When you check the budget allocation for Ward no. 33, Manorayanapalya. The numbers are staggering for the amount described and allocated for this Ward in terms of development, park infrastructures, and other celebrations.

But I do not see any active measure to curb over populating of vehicles on the road, drunkards who are abusing most of the time, throwing garbage on the streets, and when confronted, which I did and got into a physical fight. But you cannot speak to all the people who are irresponsible in their actions. That's when the people of the nation elected people come to picture. But they aren't available if there isn't anything for them in it. Yes, I agree some changes has taken effect, but it's not consistent. 

Everything is back to normal in less than a week or two. 

I urge the MLA of this constituency to install metal dustbins along with a Plant every 20 to 30 metet to avoid congestion and to talk to people about using a dustin papers to avoid spillage, foul and above all, cleanliness. Which not only helps, the people who throw them but also those who work hard to collect them. 

As far as development, all I can see is Buildings raising from the ground and the greenery being eaten alive. This is not how I Remember growing up in this place and it brings me pain and sorrow to live a life with bad air quality and a dirty neighborhood. 

The question remains, Where did all that amount go to?
location: 31, 5Th Cross Road, Vishweshwaraiah Layout, Ratnamma Layout, Chamundi Nagar, Hebbal, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560032, India

title: Junk hill @ busy Junction
description:
It is said that Bannerghatta road is one of the fast-developing roads in all of Bangalore. But the photos attached to this complaint says otherwise. Don't believe it? The spot marked on the map and the photos explaining the scene is less than 10meters from the very famous Vega City mall. In spite of being one of the busiest roads with a lot of traffic. People usually swim through this heap of garbage to cross the Jedi Mara signal. I commute every day to my office using the same road having to see such blissful sight every day of my life. I have also seen people living nearby or just on the opposite side of the road throw food/plastic/wet/dry/degradable/non-degradable wastes and create such a beautiful mystery making it hard to classify the type of waste. But enough of all the jokes and sarcasm, let's get serious for a minute. I hope the below point will give a gist of the problem. 
1. I have witnessed a 2-wheeler rider fell and got badly hurt as he got out of balance when his vehicle moved over some wet waste. 
2. This is the welcome mat for people getting down from the BTMC buses. 
3. Because of this people are not able to use footpath and are compelled to get on the road to cross the street. And even when crossing one must take a deep breath so that your olfactory nodes don't get damaged. 
4. Since this occupies half of the road, commuters can use only the other half of the road. Many a time this causes serious traffic blockage. 
5. And since this is the heart of the rainy season all the dumped garbage gets clog the drainpipes and causes rainwater to overflow on the streets. 
Taking all these points into consideration, I request the concerned team/authority to take necessary actions and solve this issue. And I also ask to impose strict rules and heavy penality for irresponsible behaviors. Thanks in advance.
location: Jp Nagara 3Rd Phase, Bannerghatta Main Road, Dollars Colony, Stage 2, J P Nagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560078, India
address: Jedi Mara bus stop. Beside Vega city Mall
sub_category_title: Clearance Of Garbage Dump Or Black Spot

title: Need to enforce Traffic lights and oneway rules strictly
description:
Kadubeesanahalli junction on top of underpass is big mess and risky for pedestrians and bikers with vehicles coming from all directions all the time.

We have traffic lights but they are not on.

We have one ways but they are not enforced strictly.

We need better management of this junction with traffic lights, strict enforment of traffic rules  and one ways.

The intersection  should be zero tolerance zone like other important junctions in the city with white markers i.e vehicles should not stand still  in junction/intersection blocking vehicles in other directions.

Vehicles should stop before zebra crossing if road ahead is blocked/jammed even if they have green signal.

If required we can even have a small island at the centre to regulate the movement of traffic.

attaching a sample photo how vehicles at the intersection can block  vehicles from other directions even though other roads are free.

title: Despite of garbage vans coming around, the sweepers dispose garba...
description:
Despite of garbage vans coming around, the sweepers dispose garbage on the road and not in the right place . Moreover not just the sweepers, I have witnessed lot of people(fruit and vegetable vendors, pedestrians, food wagon owners) throwing of garbage on along the roadsides. These garbage has a major portion of plastic (food packets and plastic bags). Disposing such waste in open gives birth to diseases.I have come across so many people living in that area suffering from maleria,dengue etc. Not only garbage waste but open gutters also act as one of the major contributors towards the spread of these diseases. These sewage gutters have become home for mosquitoes and lots of other insects. The irony of the situation is this open disposal is happening right in the area where there are lots of play school and hospitals. I request the concerned department to cover the road side sewage lines and place Dustbins to stop open garbage disposal. I also urge the department to place Sinages stating penalties for disposing in open. I am sure penalising people for irresponsible behaviour will help us in keeping a check on disposal issue. 


Please take actions on ASAP Basis.

title: Noise pollution - disturbance for the mentally disabled
description:
The large scale effects of traffic and other related day-to-day factors that cause noise can be crippling , even more so for those that are troubled with psychological defects such as Schizophrenia, Alzheimer's, Autism , and so on.
Therefore as a conscious citizen and a patient of Psychosis, I write this plea for all of the public to take a step in making an effort towards bringing a change in their civic attitudes and reducing their footprint on noise levels in our beautiful city and in turn, our mother earth. A clear, healthy mind and body are indicators of a consummate environment, so, starting of with the right intent to your life and to your neighbours, reflects as a valuable step undertaken for the cause.
location: Koramangala, Bengaluru, Karnataka, India

title: Access Road to Jal Vayu Towers Choked due to Parking on Both Sides of the Road
description:
There is a narrow road called Temple Road leading from the ESI hospital corner, behind Baiyappannahalli metro station, to Jal Vayu Towers (JVT) Apartments. Until just one year ago, the road used to be quiet and allowed easy vehicular movement. However, now the same road is always packed with vehicles parked on either side. Moreover, there are several taxis, buses, trucks, and a road roller parked there almost permanently. It is almost impossible for two 4-wheelers to cross each other, let alone a large fire engine to come through.

To add to this chaos, there is a commercial establishment called, “Maniranjan Diesel Sales and Services Limited” further up on the road that appears to have gone from a small quiet business to a large one, creating utter unruliness. Aside from several two-wheelers, there are large vehicles always double-parked in front of this establishment, either for loading or unloading, making it extremely difficult to use the road.

During development of this property Jal Vayu Towers has surrendered some of its land so that the road would be wide enough for a fire engine to come through in an emergency. However, now it seems that that was completely in vain and our fire safety is seriously jeopardized. 

We earnestly request the commissioner of Traffic Police Bangalore to investigate this matter. We recommend either making the entire street as non-parking zone with a limited number of assigned parking for the complex residents or introducing odd-even day parking ONLY on one side of the road. This is of utmost urgency for the safety of JVT residents.
location: 1/9, Temple Rd, Krishnaiyyanapalya, Bennigana Halli, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560038, India

description:

The road is in horrible condition. Since past 25 years this road is not being tarred. title: Removal of unauthorized speedbreakers
description:
Dear Sir/Ma'am,

This is bring to your notice that there has been unauthorized construction of speed-breakers on Medahalli - Kadugodi road/ Belathur main road. There are more than two dozen excessively high speed-breakers on this stretch of just five kms placed at about every 50 metres, which are scraping both two wheelers and four wheelers alike. Not to mention that this stretch is already riddled with numerous potholes and recently the road has been dug up in multiple places by BWSSB for underground drainage work and is left as it is after work completion. I've to drive my old parents on this road everyday on two wheeler and it gets extremely uncomfortable and bone jarring risky experience. There have been many instances of people on two wheelers getting injured due these unauthorized speed-breakers and potholes riddled road everyday. This issue was covered in the news as well in November 2019, However no action has been taken as yet. Please see below link for your easy reference.

https://www.newindianexpress.com/cities/bengaluru/2019/nov/14/residents-of-belathur-in-bengaluru-say-a-prayer-before-hitting-the-roads-2061514.html 

Could you please look into the issue and remove the unauthorized speed-breakers and neutralize the excessively high ones, also patch up the dug-up roads so that it becomes safe and comfortable for the residents and people who ply on this road every day. 

Your timely response in this regard would be highly appreciated! 
Many Thanks for your consideration. 

Regards, 
Utkarsh Jain
location: Sonnenahalli, Krishnarajapura, Bengaluru, Karnataka, India

title:
Hello,
I am living in choodasandra, near nandini circle. we absol...
description:
Hello,
I am living in choodasandra, near nandini circle. we absolutely don't have waste management here, when I initially shifted I bought 2 dustbin, one for wet and the other for dry, but we had absolutely none coming to collect, after few days I was forced to follow what others are doing that is throw the garbage, both wet and dry together on the side of the road. It really breaks my heart to do this every week... I carry cotton bags everywhere, try to avoid swiggy or Uber as much as possible, but certain things are unavoidable.... and absolutely no way of disposal for the waste, please do make this your highest priority and help us keep our surroundings clean... of course there are other problems too, we Don have roads, problem with drinking water but this is unbearable and causing a lot of issues. I am also attaching an image of the same, we have such dumping spots on both the sides of the road and it continues for kilometers... Hopefully this will be addressed.

title: Underpass badly designed and inadequate
description:
The railway underpass is so badly designed that crossing this takes more than 30 minutes. There are many issues with the design and I wonder how this design was even approved. The entrances have 90 degree sharp edges with high drain covers, How many vehicles on earth can turn 90 degrees. The entrance from ITI  main gate to Whitefield main road has empty space on left that is used by BMTC buses and this empty land allows opportunist drivers to make upto 10 lanes all merging to 1 lane causing huge jam everyday.
So please make arrnagements for ensuring that no extra lanes are possible so that jams can be avoided.
Remove/move accordingly the height barriers at the entrance from KR Puram side and make the edges curved, so that it becomes easy for vehicles to turn.
All these should be seen as temporary fix, as this kind of roads cannot be permanent for connect three major roads (Old Madras Road, Whitefield Road and ORR).
The civic authorities should use some logic and expertise to look into solving this road condition. There should eitther be multi level flyover or underpass. No more jugad will work at this junction.
Please look into this issue as this has impact to lakhs of peoples' daily commute.

title: I was a single lady at 9PM, driver and conductor asked me get down from the bus as they were not interested to go till drop point.
description:
HI,

I'm swathi working in MNC company in electronic city, i have been using NICE 2A bus since 1 year. I was the last person to get down because the last drop was till Uttarahalli from Electronic city. 7PM bus, every time the conductor used to ask me to get down near konankunte cross as he is not interested to finish his duty till Uttarahalli as i was the only one person to be dropped.  In the month of Jan 2020, i took NICE 2A 7PM bus, the conductor gave the ticket till Uttarahalli that is 40 rupees, but he asked me to get down near konanakunte cross because he didnot want to go till Uttarahalli as i was the only one lady. i told him that i cannot get down because im not well and you have given me the ticket till Uttarahalli. title: Complaint regarding Non-cleaning of Garbage and drainage in our locality.
description:
Sir,
We regret to bring to your kind notice that the Garbage container of our locality is overflowing due to non-cleaning for many days. The rotten waste materials emanating bad smell thus making the people of the nearby area to wear a scarf around their nose. If the same condition prevails for few more days then there will be a chance of spreading disease. The condition is becoming worse day by day. We, the resident of VS Reddy Colony, 2nd Cross, Patalamma Layout, Kadugodi, Whitefield  un-aware of the reason why municipality neglected our area. In addition to this, the drainage also gets blocked in several places. So, if rain occurs than it may create water logging problem too.
for the past two weeks, the garbage truck has not even passed our housing area, photos are attached for your reference. 
As a result, there is a mountain of rubbish in front of our houses. The garbage is swarming with flies and has become the breeding place for maggots. In addition, stray dogs & pigs would come and mess with the rubbish, tearing the garbage bags into pieces and spreading the refuse all over the place.
Worst of all, the garbage is now is now emitting a foul smell and is giving our neighborhood a bad image.
Therefore, It's our humbly request that the same may kindly be cleaned by today itself so that we can have a normal life soon.
Thanking you,
Yours faithfully,
Resident of VS Reddy Colony, 2nd Cross, 
Patalamma Layout, Kadugodi, Whitefield
location: 10, Patalamma Second Cross, Near Ever Green Mart, V S Reddy Colony, Kadugodi, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560067, India
address: Near Euro kids play home or Every Green mart
sub_category_title: Clearance Of Garbage Dump Or Black Spot

title: Huge pile of Garbage thrown on the road
description:
There is a huge pile of garbage on N.P Factory Road (near to Pacific Residency  Apartment).   I understand that people in the locality and also people from nearby market (including non veg waste) throw the waste in the same place.  This poses serious threat to all children and citizens who are residing in the near by locality.  We are literally not able to open up our windows or doors on the main road due to the unbearable and horrendous stench that emanates from this garbage.  Almost half of the road is piled up with garbage and these people who do not follow the law just go on piling the garbage on top of it every day.  We are forced to close down all windows and doors for 24 hrs in a day which leads to suffocation inside our home.  If we open it then we are exposed to unfortunate infections and diseases that may come out of this menace.  The problem has reached a peak now wherein we are just unable to bear this stench and fearing every day what disease might come to us due to this careless behavior of throwing garbage on the roads of few people.  

Therefore I request your kind attention, help and support to immediately take action and enforce some strict action to ensure the road becomes clean without garbage anymore...We have great faith and confidence in the capacity of the BBMP officials and the government and we sincerely hope and trust that this problem will be acted upon soon and strict measures taken to avoid any future repetition of throwing garbage in this road.  Request you to pls save us from this situation....
location: Kaval Bairasandra, Bengaluru, Karnataka, India
address: N.P Factory Road (opp to Kaval Byrasandra Bus stand) / Next to Pacific Residency Apartment & DS Max Apartment

title: Request to repair Road breakdown and strengthening.
description:
Respected Sir/Mam,

I am a resident of above address from past 5+ years. Our one end of the road connects to Magadi main road and the other end connects to the parallel neighbor road. Daily from past 1.5yrs we are facing serious problems because of the parallel connecting road breakdown. Now only one end of the road is being used which is usually crowded by bikes, Autos, Ambulance, Buses and people waiting for buses.
This is causing roadblocks when 2 cars approach in the opposite direction where one had to stop until the other vehicle passes.
And many of the vehicles like JCB, Sanitary vehicles face problem to take U-turn. They will have to go across each house to see whether they have enough space to take U-turn. Earlier Cars and Autos used the parallel connecting road to move on to the neighboring roads but now they will have to wait and carefully go through main road as the bus stop is right at the entry of the road.
Due to this pandemic everyone in the resident are using their vehicles in only one end of the road which is creating congestion.
Many people had fallen down using the parallel connecting road because of its current condition and its even more worse during rainy days.
Kindly help to correct the road breakdown and its strengthening.

Regards,
Jagadeep

A lot of times our sleep is disrupted because of their irritating sound on holidays.

Now since the pandemic has started the number of street hawkers have increased which is good in a way, but inspiring from that one street hawker now multiple street hawkers have started to use loudspeakers and the nuisance of sound pollution has increased and it's almost continuous over the day. 

A lot of times these vendors are talking/yelling over the mike 2 or 3 people at a time such that we do not even understand who is saying what and get so irritated with the sound that we have to close our house windows, which is not a practical option.

I request you to please ask the street hawkers to not use the loudspeakers and just use their voice if they want to sell their goods.

Thank you.

title: Noise Pollution
description:
We have knocked on the doors of local police and also politicians but till date we have not received any positive outcome for our expressed concerns. Hope at least there shall be some action taken here.

We are residents of 3rd A Cross, New Venkatapura, Koramangala 1st Block, Bangalore-560034. 

#309/1, Ground and 1st floor Building. This building is located in a residential place and we are the neighbours of that building. The owner of the building has rented out one of the shops to an Metal Industrial Work. Every single day we are facing lots and lots of noise pollution because of this shop from the last 6-8 months. Despite highlighting this to the local authorities nobody is bothered to take any action.

isn't there a demarcation of Residential and Industrial places Sir? How are we supposed to stay at home with the loud noise of metal cutting and hammering every single day. This starts every day from morning 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM. Loud noise of Metal cutting, Metal Hammering, Welding Gas Pollution. We are not able to sit at home, our kids are not able to study, and old people are suffering every single day because of this noise & welding Gas pollution. Despite us staying in a residential area, we feel as if we are now staying in some Industrial area.

with a lot of hopes we are posting our complaint here, we hope our complaint will not go unnoticed as it has gone with Police and other authorities.

Building owner's name is Gangadhar and his mobile number is +91 96329 03602.

Building Address: #309/1, Ground and 1st floor Building, 3rd cross, 2nd Main, New Venkatapura, Koramangala 1st Block

title: House owners torture with tenants
description:
Respected sir, 

I am a Naturopathic and Homeopathic Doctor and have my clinical service in HBR layout, 4th Block, Hennur.  

I was staying in this house since January 2019 and the agreement was executed for the Rs.60000 Security Deposit by the house owner, after 3 months the building owner regretted giving me water and not allowed me to wash and dry my clothes in the open space of the last floor. And harassing and abusing me using bad words and telling me to leave Karnataka and go to your home state. And also many issues by building owners two sons.  

Regarding these torture i have lodged a complaint in court and the civil case is on progress. Also i stopped giving rent because of no peace and not giving water also not allowing me to park my bike in his area and peaceless life.  

After the complaint the house owner's son Akshay was following me unnecessarily and i am scared to face their rudeness behaviors. Whenever i was walking on the road and when i was in my office the building owners son Akshay was harassing me to pay rent and abusing by bad words. They were opening the windows without my knowledge and sometimes they used to open the house door even when I was in the home. Because they have additional keys for the main door. I have mentioned everything in the court. Still the hearings are progress.  

Now again the house owner and his son started doing the same issues, from 07-08-2020 around 3pm house owner and his son akshay and their team visited the building and locked the water supply pipe of the water tank. I am very weak to question or request my needs with the owner. 

I am afraid of the survey in Bangalore. They already threatened me of murdering plans by their rowdyism teams.  I don't have any other option to seek help, so I request you to help me with this issue. I pray with you to protect my life from victims. 

Name of the House Owner: Neela Rani

title: Lots of cows on road, cow urine/dung smell, mosquitoes, slippery road, road block.
description:
COW MENACE

The area I am referring to belongs to Ward No. 18 they call it Gundutopu in local language.   This is near AECS Community hall, near Adarsh Prabhakar Gold Arch Apartment, behind Toshniwal Bros Co.,  it is on the same road as that of "Friends Apartment".  The proper address is, Nandikeshwara Nilaya, no. 6, AECS Layout Ist Stage, RMV IInd Stage, Geddalahalli, Bangalore 560094.

There are dozens of stray cows that stay on the road.  During the daytime, they go out roaming and assemble in the evening wherein cowman milks the cow.  From evening to morning, all cows stay on the road itself and some calves (baby cows) stay all along. The road is filled with cow dung, cow urine, and dirty materials.  They smell so bad.  The cow's dug is of loose stool consistency with dirty unbearable smell.  Cow owners do not care any despite we have complained them on to the police.  They push cow-related waste to the drain.  Drain is with full of cow's urine and dung materials leading to insects including mosquitoes.  Cow owners milk the cow at the center of the road, and they do not even leave the way to by-passers.  They leave the cow dung wherever it is and do not bother to clear up.  Many motor vehicles have skid and injured because of it.  During the night, cows sleep on the road itself and do not give the way to the vehicles.  At night, cows lay its dung in front of our gate itself and many a times, we had stepped on it and had to wash shoes evenly to clear it from the smell. When our vehicle rolls on them, they carry dirt to our parking area with foul smell.

This is a long-pending issue, and I have attached various photographs related to this.

Eagerly anticipating your early action and permanent resolution.
location: 11, Rb Rd, Met Layout, Geddalahalii, Armane Nagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560094, India
address: On the road of Friends apartment, behind Toshniwal Company, near Adarsh Prabhakar Gold Arch Apartment.
sub_category_title: Controlling Of Stray Cattle Or Maintenance Of Cattle Pound

title: Water Problem
description:
I reside in 8th cross Dasappa layout 3rd block. Facing severe water problems since 9 months inspite of heavy rainfall in the state this year.

we are not getting sufficient water both from CMC borewell and Cauvery inspite of multiple complaints to valve men and BWSSB office.

there are 16-17 houses in this cross, about 10 houses have given commission to valve men and put valves in front of their houses and they are regularly getting CMC water throughout the day and not having need for cauvery water and 4 houses are regularly taking 3 -4 water tankers every single month.

cauvery water is supplied on Mon and Thursday but the valve would keep the pressure high for 1 hour and reduce for next hour so its not sufficient for us .

My question is if all of us are paying taxes why are only some houses getting water all day while others have to buy water tankers inspite of paying cauvery and borewell charges. Who has given permission to dug the tar road without no objection certificate from the BBMP and make changes to the water lines to fulfil few houses needs and burden others, I want thorough investigation on the water lines to ensure justice for everyone.
location: Dasappa Layout, Ramamurthy Nagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560016, India
address: Opp Kanti sweets, Dasappa layout 8th cross

description:
Respected SirMadam,
Over the past one week, the quality of air has deteriorated increasingly. There's a very strong rotting smell, it appears that it could be because of an array of factories that is amongst our residential area of Dr Shivaramakaranth Nagar. We suspect that it could be because of huge wet waste dumps in and around the area. I have notified the PCB (pollution control board) and BBMP to investigate. To better increase the chance of find the root cause, I've the attached a map of the residents that primarily get this smell. It is particular. 
This smell has been persistent and getting stronger by the day, the kids in the house are suffering from itchiness in the throat by breathing this foul air and since we're not aware of the health hazards of this toxic smell, we want your help to avoid any harm caused by this. We request officials of the pollution control board and BBMP to please come and inspect the location. Please help the taxpaying and law-abiding citizens of Winnfield garden. Your help is much appreciated. Residents in other parts of the city have also complained of smell like this in the past. https://www.newindianexpress.com/cities/bengaluru/2019/oct/03/stink-from-e-city-plant-keeps-residents-up-at-night-2042663.html & https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/city/bengaluru/bengaluru-stench-torments-whitefield-residents/articleshow/78943488.cms

title: K Narayanapura Main Road in worst condition, full of potholes and dust. Also connecting road to New airport road of Kothnur.
description:
Respected Sir/Madam,
It’s been over 2 years that the road between K Narayanapura cross and Kothnur & Hennur(K Narayanpura Main Road) has been in a pathetic state. This stretch of road is so bad that it has severe backaches to PREGNANT women and several others of middleold aged men who use this road to travel. Needless to say, the dust that this road generates have aggravated respiratory diseases in many who live & work around here. 
This area is occupied by diligent tax payers, it is a SHAME that this road in this kind of WORST condition and that too for more than 2 years now! Do we citizens not deserve good roads? Don’t we deserve good quality of life? Least of all, don’t deserve basic facilities like GOOD BREATHING AIR and GOOD ROAD INFRASTRUCTURE. It is thoroughly frustrating that irritants like these are causing the youth to LEAVE this country and never look back. To think beyond corruption and selfishness is required for a sustainable future. Do it for your own children, for they may forsake you for the dirty environment that we’ve created and move to another country.
Please be considerate to us citizens and fix these roads. With all sincerity, I request BBMP & other respectable authorities to fix the K Narayanapura cross (Please do not subject to patchyshoddy work, request to fixtar the road properly thereby getting rid of the dust as well). We look forward to your response and hope to together make Bengaluru a better place to live.
Regards,
Vikas Hiremath
location: K Narayanapura Main Rd, Bds Nagar, Narayanapura, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560077, India

title: Suggestion for Clean Bangalore
description:
There are many vacant sites in our Layout and also in entire BBMP limits. The owners of this sites are not at all taking care or visit these sites. Many sites are turned into dump yards and senseless people are throwing plastic waste/all type of garbage on these sites and garbage dumps are formed on many sites. Many a times I have picked up plastic waste and handed over to BBMP garbage vans. A lot of unwanted bushes and vegetation grown on these sites are not cleaned which gives scope for snakes and rodents to make their home. Some sites are being used for illegal activities at nights. Vacant Site owners are not bothered. Problem is only for those who have constructed homes in between and residing there.
I request you to forward my following suggestion to concerned higher authorities, which will resolve the vacant site menace and also create revenue for BBMP and make Bangalore clean.
Most of these sites are purchased for investment purpose and those owners are comfortably staying in nice homes in other parts of Bengaluru. If any person who is already possessing a home or having a site in his name or in the name of his spouse in BBMP limits, and if he has any vacant site in Bengaluru, then a time limit of say 1 or 2 years to be given for any construction activity on that vacant site. If it is not done, then as a penalty, BBMP or Govt can charge property tax at ten times the normal tax, and maintain such sites, neatly. Alternatively, by  amending the related laws, Govt can take over such sites by paying a compensation at the rate of purchase price plus indexation benefit. 
Land is a scarce commodity, and Govt can not allow anyone to hoard it. Land price is jacked up only due to hoarding. As many politicians are holding huge vacant land in BBMP limits,I am aware that my suggestion will not be looked into. But I believe that some sincere officials in BBMP apply their mind to above suggestion which is source of revenue to BBMP and make my Bengaluru neat and clean.
location: Bgs Layout, 8Th Phase, J. P. Nagar, Kothnur, Karnataka 560076, India

title: Scarcity and inconsistency of water supply for several weeks
description:
Dear Sir/Madam,
This is the second time I am rising a complaint about scarcity of water supply to our locality Ramamurthy Nagar, Yerranapalya 8th cross. I am escalating our pain points on behalf of our locality people.

Last 5 weeks before when we raised the complaint, we believe you had taken action and we have had sufficient water supply provided for just 2 weeks. Now for the last 3 and half weeks, we are struggling with insufficient water supply. When we all spoke to the concerned person (Seena) he mentioned that there is no water supply provided by the government.

Every time we speak to him, he gives us different days and time as a water supply schedule and it never happens. Last week he told to all of us that every Monday, Wednesday and Saturday will be the water supply and for 3 hours of each date. But, 
we don’t see it happening in practice.

Either the BWSSB is not providing enough water supply or this individual (Seena) is highly irresponsible in serving his duties as a public servant or potential misuse of the water supply illegally. On any of the cases, it is a serious issue and as an end result, we general public are suffering from water scarcity.

Before we interfere respective political leaders, social media and media to escalate this issue, we believe that you will address this issue for long term with high priority.

Request you to interfere respective supervisor or management to come and visit our place, hear our difficulties and resolve the issue with all respect. Also request you to assign a responsible public servant to handle basic needs of the public.

Your kind support would be much appreciated.

Many thanks in advance.
Dr.Sathish.S

title: Garbage Dumping In Front of our house
description:
Dear Sir/Madam,

Sub: Garbage Dumping In Front of our house

Good Morning, I am resident of KalyanNagar (behind Speciality Hospital), we HAVE being complaining that people are dumping the trash in front of our house, with COVID and other virus raising their heads, we request YOU to take measures to ensure people don’t throw the trash here…. 

They come in cars, commercial vehicles and bikes to throw the trash there, when we stop them they say the garbage is already there! There is a SACK for over 2 years, when clearing the team leaves it behind under the tree, never clearing it, and the UNCIVIL people living around  just come and throw the garbage there! 

We don't have garbage collection vehicle coming regularly to pick the trash, NEED clearance/ garbage collection vehicle regularly. The street sweepers don’t sweep… the garbage vehicle backs up in front of our gate littering the garbage. They come and cut all tree and plants in the name of clearing, but I wonder if they plant the trees that give us the oxygen we breath. This problem needs to be solved intelligently, the BandAid Method will not work. Need finite solutions.

So some 50 house in this area are dumping the garbage in front of my house. I can help with solution but need you (BBMP) to cooperate to ensure this gets resolved. Please do the needful. Leaders MUST lead by example. Hope the commissioner is interested to lead by example.

PS: We appreciate that with our continuous verbal complaints there has been clearing out multiple times sometimes, but we do need to ensure the dumping is completely stopped. We are ready to do our bit, but need the BBMP support to ensure this is done and final. NO More Dumping in front of our house.

Attachment: Yes

Regards
Christabell
Kalyannagar OMBR layout
Bangalore 560043
location: 715, 8Th Main Rd, Hrbr Layout 1St Block, Hrbr Layout, Kalyan Nagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560043, India
sub_category_title: Clearance Of Garbage Dump Or Black Spot

title: Absurd Noise Pollution caused by Cart Vendors
description:
I've lived in several areas of Bangalore, including Indiranagar, Whitefield, and Kammanahalli, and my particular area of RT Nagar is the most unnecessarily noise-polluted part of Bangalore I've encountered. The cause is cart-pushing vendors who use loudspeakers, and these are usually recordings that are on an endless loop. 

When I moved here two years ago, there were two or three vendors who used loudspeakers; currently, they number about 20. 

Two important points:
1) I am not opposed to cart vendors making a living in this or any other area; most of them simply use their natural voice to call out once every 10 or 20 meters. That's fine. Loudspeakers--particularly those with recordings on an endless loop--are unnecessary noise pollution that are a complete lack of consideration for the neighborhood. Many of us have to work online, conducting meetings and teaching classes, and banana salesmen using loudspeaker recordings should not have priority over the tasks of others who must work at home. 
2) My understanding is that these loudspeakers are already illegal in Bangalore, as I've read that any kind of public address system in this city must be at a volume that cannot be audible for more than 200 meters. I would like to see this law enforced, as this area is suffering from a great deal of unnecessary noise pollution that is impeding the productivity and peace of mind of those of us who work at home and those who simply want to enjoy a day of quietude.

In addition to the illegality and rudeness of these loudspeakers, I'll conclude by observing that many of these endless-loop recordings in my area are absolutely ABSURD in regard to the way they sound: It's as though the vendors deliberately created recordings that sound ridiculous, obnoxious, and agitating...and inexplicably believing such would attract a happy clientele.
location: Rt Nagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560032, India

title: Roadside Drain and footpath sinking
description:
Recently there was BBMP work to remove silt from the roadside drains, repair the side walls, lay top slabs with filling gaps and lay the Kerb stones. But the work did not happen as they were supposed to do. The contractor has asked the worker only to lay the Kerb stones and paint it without removing the upper stone and checking for silt or broken drain walls. When the workers were asked to check the silt and the walls they said that they were informed to only lay the Kerb stones and make up the gaps on top slabs (picture attached of worker applying cement coat on existing layer). Now the Roadside gutter and footpath (top slabs and cement) on top of it is sinking in and there is no one to complaint about it to action on as they don't care.

Attached are the photos and video of the broken roadside drain walls. Also there is one opening left without cover which was raised as a concern since many years but none have taken any action or even have courtesy to come and check.

Please look into this issue and help to rectify it as there are children walking and playing around. Hoping this will be rectified ASAP. Praying god that no major incidents happen by the time is it repaired.

title: Bull-Cow menace
description:
There are a large number of bulls, cows and calves that keep walking the streets of Teacher's colony, which is next to Venkatapura on one side and the Sarjapur Main Road on the other. 

In fact last year we helped with the birthing of a calf on our Street - 3rd Avenue out of love and compassion for the animals.  However, it seems to be posing a huge hazard to the citizens here.  Last Saturday, there were a bunch of bulls chasing a cow and to escape them I stepped on to the foot path and tripped and fell down and hurt myself very badly.  Luckily I did not fracture any bone in my body.  After this incident I also learnt from my friend who lives in the same area that she was caught in a similar situation and had broken her leg.  A couple of months ago I was returning home and at the Venkatapura junction a white bull and a black one were fighting on the road and came charging towards my vehicle.  Luckily some men in the shops came out and chased them away.  

All the streets are littered with dung, one night during a power cut and heavy rain, my maid accidentally stepped on the dung and fell down on the road. Imagine slipping and falling down on the road with dung all over you in a city like Bangalore.

These situations are really scary, it is playing with the lives of the citizens and their well being.  There are little kids playing on the streets, just imagine  a bunch of bulls chasing a cow at that time.  It will surely lead to a stampede and even death of the little ones. Before any major incident occurs this menace has to be brought under control and the animals be rounded off and not be let out on the streets once again.  

These are stray animals and belong to none.  Hence the authorities have to take up steps on a war footing to stop this menace.  Let us not wait for a tragic incident to hit the headlines and then wake up and take measures to do something about it.
location: Teacher's Colony, Hsr Layout 5Th Sector, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560034, India
sub_category_title: Controlling Of Stray Cattle Or Maintenance Of Cattle Pound

title: Garbage Vehicle parking and segregation taking place on open roads
description:
Greetings, 
I am a student who regularly walks between Ramesh Nagar and Old Airport road because of poor connectivity of public transport within 2Km of my house, the walk is extremely unpleasant because of a few reasons. The most important one being the fact that garbage segregation takes place on open roads which leave a nasty odor for anyone walking. It is also a problem that many HAL mini buses are parked on both sides of the road rendering the already narrow footpaths useless and pushing pedestrians onto the roads with lots of turns and high speed traffic. I request at least one of the following to be implemented.
Better bus(or at least midi bus) connectivity and frequency near annasandra palya and basavanagar regions of high population density.
Proper yards for parking of garbage vehicles and segregation of wastes.
Clean, wide and well lit footpaths from ramesh nagar to Old airport road.
By no means am i an expert, but i am a citizen giving my complaints and what i think may solve these problems.
Thank you
location: 13Th Cross Rd, Hal Gb Quaters, Vibhutipura, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560037, India

title: Deadly potholes with no sign of road at all
description: Basapura road towards Niladri is full of potholes with no sign of road at all. Every year BBMP repair the road in the monsoon season which is not the ideal time to recarpet from any logic. It washes away again in the next 2 months. This is a waste of time and resources of govt. All heavy trucks which should not be allowed on this single-lane road are freely allowed in front of traffic police and they simply don't care. Because of all those trucks no matter how many times govt re-carpets the road it always ends up in potholes every 2 months. Because of all the deadly potholes and heavy trucks passing through this single-lane road it's almost impassable and very risky to ride two-wheeler. If govt wants to allow trucks on this road then make sure to create the road of NH quality. Because every minute at least 5 heavy trucks cross this road throughout the day. When it comes to collecting fines from the public for all silly reasons Bangalore traffic police is always ready but when it comes to fixing roads they close their eyes.

title:
Dear authorities, 

Dasarahalli, Bengaluru, 


I am writing to yo...
description:
Dear authorities, 

Dasarahalli, Bengaluru, 


I am writing to you as a vote casting citizen to kindly draw your attention to the terrible condition of the roads in my neighborhood - Kirloskar Layout. We have been peaceful residents of this layout since the year 2007, but our roads were dug up first in the year 2018 for sanitation purposes. It has been nearly 4 years since and no attempt has been made to cover the roads up again. 


We suffer lots of dust in our homes during summers and terrible water clogged potholes in monsoon. Senior citizens and children alike face a lot of difficulty walking on them. Riding a two wheeler also proves difficult and dangerous. 


Despite our trying to bring this to your office's attention multiple times in the past through our Shri Maruti Mahila Sangha (Rgd), no effort in this direction has been made whatsoever. I request you to please do the needful at the earliest and sincerely hope you take our words into account this time.


Please find enclosed - pictures of our roads in their present condition.


Thanking you in anticipation.
location: Kirloskar Layout, Soundarya Layout, Bengaluru, Karnataka, India
sub_category_title: Tarring Or Asphalting Of Mud/Kutcha/Unpaved Road

title: Road and Park Encroachment
description:
There is a peaceful layout in JP Nagar 9th Phase by name Royal Lake Front Residency - Phase 3 (South). Recently BBMP had invested millions of Rupees in developing the park with new plantations and lawn after residents request for development of the same. The residents including children and senior citizens were using the park extensively for walking and recreation. This park was the breathing space with lot of greenery and as per BDA approved layout  plan .  Adjacent to this park there was a road which was connecting Raghavana Palya to adjacent BDA Layout and further areas. Such being the fact, all of a sudden on 10th July 2021, the adjacent landlord of Survey No. 24 (The farm house in the name of Avadhani Estate), Raghavana Palya, Uttarhalli Hobli, Bangalore South Taluk by name Smt. Saraswathi w/o of CV Nagesh have entered the park area along with 50 to 60 people with material and earth movers and forcefully demolished the compound which is adjacent to the road as discussed above without giving any opportunity to speak. The said Smt.  Saraswathi has brought few officials who were influenced by her and supported the demolition of the wall of public property. The officials who were influenced by her has not allowed the general public to speak or discuss on the illegal encroachment of the park and road. The officials forcefully threaten the residents that if they intervene in the demolition, they will put the residents behind the bars.

This cart road is existed from way long back and the same is marked in the village map and sketch also. The neighbouring house owner by name Mr. Joseph has constructed a wall on the other end of the cart road and used the road area as his private property. Mr. Joseph has also constructed a compound wall restricted the movement of the residents and has encroached the public property. The photos the same has been uploaded for better understanding.
The complaints to Tahsildar, Bangalore South, Deputy Commission, Bangalore, Revenue Minister, Principal Secretary Revenue Department, Horticulture Department, BBMP Parks, Registrar Karnataka Land Grabbing Prohibition special court, ADGP BMTF has been given immediately after the incident has been taken place. On the day of incident Tahsildar who was influenced by the landlord was also present at the spot and supporting the illegal encroachment. It is very unfortunate that none of the above departments / officials have initiated any legal action to stop the illegalities and take action/punish the law breakers and encroachers.
Frustrated by all these illegalities and no official supported, this illegality with a last hope, we are failing this in this forum to see if some justice or public pressure will create to bring back the public property from the encroached persons.
location: Rlf Phase 3 South Park, 7Th Cross Rd, Royal Lakefront Residency Phase 3, 8Th Phase, J. P. Nagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560062, India

Also the PUB is run till late night, due to parties there is lots of sound.

The staff start cleaning utensils in late night making lots of sound. This also has been informed many a times to shift the cleaning area but again the PUB management never looks for a solution to it.

title: Footpath
description:
The footpath from the junction of Lavelle Rd/Vittal Mallya Rd near Canara Bank towards Dsouza Layout/Ganjam Jewellers is being systematically destroyed by BBMP/UB City/Prestige Builders under the guise of cleaning the storm water drain running below the footpath.( work has been on going since past one month starting in early February). The tiles laid are being removed in order to uncover the manhole covers resulting in broken tiles which are not being replaced,as well as resulting in further damage to the adjacent tiles.
The existing  manhole covers of granite slabs have been broken/damaged while trying to open the manholes for cleaning of the drain.The damaged and badly broken/cracked granite covers have been haphazardly re positioned with the result that if an unsuspecting pedestrian steps on the cover,he may seriously injure himself if the cover gives way and he falls into the drain.
The mud and debris removed from the drain has also been piled up along side the pavement and not been cleared since the past one month.

UB City Manager /engineer in charge has been informed,however no action has been taken as yet to rectify the damages to the pavement tiles and manhole covers.

The damaged granite covers need to be replaced with proper galvanized hinged steel covers for easy acess in future when ever cleaning of the drains is required to be carried out.

Pl see the attached photographs.
location: Vittal Mallya Rd, Shanthala Nagar, Ashok Nagar, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560001
address: Along side Garden Apartments compound wall starting from Canara Bank upto Dsouza Layout
sub_category_title: Build new footpaths and repair broken footpaths

title: Footpath
description:
The stretch of Tumkur Road in the T.Dasarahalli area about which there is this controversy whether the BBMP or the Highway Authority should handle has posed lot of risk to pedestrians. Inspite of having a four lane road, there is absolutely no bifurcation of the service road and foot path. After the construction of the incomplete four lane, construction material and debris has not been levelled. It's like trekking with ups and downs in the space alloted for footpath. Right from rockline mall at jalahalli cross to 8th mile it's a mess especially for children and senior citizens using this path to walk upto jalhalli cross bus stand and fro to Dasarahalli. People have to negotiate between autos and two wheelers and sometimes four wheelers too. So also on the other side of Tumkur road from jalahalli cross upto Kanti sweets in most places there is an edge for the footpath with a row of shops almost in the stilt.
And with two and three  wheelers Parked, it's almost like falling down from the edge of the footpath. So also the pedestrian crossing at Dasarahalli, near Metro station, four roads are tobe crossed. There are barricades between the fourlanes that has tobe negotiated amidst big flock of pedestrians waiting to cross. The pedestrians signal time is less compared to the no.of people crossing. We have to see if removing of barricades help or make it worst.
location: Tumkur Rd, Yeshwanthpur Industrial Area, Phase 1, Yeswanthpur, Bengaluru, Karnataka
address: From jalahalli cross to Dasarahalli Metro station on either side of Tumkur road
sub_category_title: Build new footpaths and repair broken footpaths

title: Park - BDA PARK NO: 18
description:
There is a barren land near Kodi Singasandra lake Which was allocated fo BDA PARK NO:18. As this land is adjacent to the lake, It makes it an ideal spot for a park. The area is huge 7 has lot of trees as well.
A couple of months back, fencing was done by the authorities  & signboards were placed mentioning "BDA Park No: 18". We all were very happy. But suddenly before the park work could start it was suddenly repainted as "Local Playground" overnight because of unknown reasons.  
There is no park in the radius of 4kms. The park, when opened in the allocated area, can be used by more than 7000 families living in a radius of 2 km around the area. All these families will be grateful to you If the park is opened at the earliest. I'm writing this on behalf of 83 families living in Yuva Eka apartment. Kindly request you to take this as our appeal requesting a park in the allocated area.

Since then, I have been regularly complaining to BESCOM's helpline (which too has been sending reminder mails to the concerned dept. to take suitable action), as well as to BESCOM officials, but to no avail. There has been no response, let alone any activity, whatsoever.

title: Water flowing on street like a river
description:
I just faced a near death experience. There is only one way out to the city for nearly 5000 residents in spice garden layout. Water flows out of a manhole on one end of the street only to flow into a canal at the other end of the street. The place where it flows into the canal is a deadly place with no Street lights, at least half a feet deep water and open flow into the canal. 

Just now i tried coming through it on my bike and i had to stop suddenly as a car stopped infront of me. I put my leg on the side for support only to find it was entrance to the canal and my whole leg slipped into the canal. Bike weight is over me and i couldn't get up. Luckily some people lifted the bike and saved me. 

This happens everytime there is a strong rain. Almost all the 5000 residents in this colony are employees who are paying lakhs of rupees in taxes every year. But even then there is no basic safety for all these residents. 

If wearing a non ISI certified helmet it dangerous and attracts a fine of 500/- then how dangerous this situation is and multiply that by 5000. Why aren't police collecting this fine from whoever is responsible for maintaining these roads??
location: Spice Garden Layout, Lakshminarayana Pura, Aecs Layout, Chinnapanna Halli, Bengaluru, Karnataka 560037, India
